title: Caramelized Chewy Thai Chicken Satay
description: A Whole30-friendly, fermented, and chewy Thai dish perfect for a date night, featuring caramelized flavors and unflavored gelatin. Marinated chicken breast is grilled to perfection and served with a side of fresh vegetables.
instructions: In a small bowl, whisk together gelatin and 1/4 cup of cold water to soften.
In a blender or food processor, combine coconut milk, fish sauce, lime juice, honey, ginger, curry paste, salt, and pepper. Blend until smooth.
Add the gelatin mixture to the blender and blend until well combined.
Place the chicken strips in a large bowl and pour the marinade over them. Mix well to coat and cover the bowl with plastic wrap.
Refrigerate for at least 2 hours or overnight to allow the chicken to ferment and become chewy.
Preheat a grill or grill pan to medium-high heat. Thread the marinated chicken onto skewers, if desired.
Grill the chicken for 5-7 minutes per side, or until cooked through and slightly charred.

While the chicken is cooking, prepare a caramel sauce by heating 1/4 cup of coconut milk in a small saucepan over medium heat. Bring to a simmer and cook for 5-7 minutes, or until the sauce has thickened and caramelized.
Serve the grilled chicken with the caramel sauce spooned over the top and garnished with fresh cilantro and vegetables, if desired.
